task_completion.description: Open and read the family assessment JSON file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor, terminal, or file viewer displaying the contents of /home/ga/Documents/family_assessment.json.

task_completion.description: Create the document with a title and an auto-generated Table of Contents
task_completion.visual_evidence: A word processor (e.g., LibreOffice Writer) is open, showing a document with a title and a Table of Contents block at the top (often indicated by a gray background for auto-generated fields).

task_completion.description: Format sections using Heading 1 and Heading 2 styles
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text in the document is formatted as headings, and the styles dropdown/sidebar in the word processor explicitly shows 'Heading 1' or 'Heading 2' being applied to sections like Family ID, Case Info, Goals, etc.

task_completion.description: Insert at least three tables for Goals, Services, and Visitation
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document contains at least three distinct tables organizing the Goals, Service Referrals, and Visitation Schedule.

task_completion.description: Add page numbers to the document footer
task_completion.visual_evidence: The footer area at the bottom of the document pages contains page numbers.

task_completion.description: Include accurate family and case data from the source file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document text contains specific details from the JSON, such as the name 'Martinez', case number 'JC-2024-0847-JCFC', or service providers like 'Centerstone' and 'Volunteers of America'.

task_completion.description: Save the document as an ODT file in the correct location
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog shows the filename 'Martinez_Family_Case_Plan.odt' being saved in the /home/ga/Documents/ directory, or the file is visible in a file manager at that path.

integrity.description: The agent used a GUI word processor to create the document
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with a graphical word processor like LibreOffice Writer, rather than using terminal commands or a code editor to write raw XML/ODT files.

integrity.description: The Table of Contents and page numbers are auto-generated fields
integrity.visual_evidence: The Table of Contents and page numbers have a gray background (in LibreOffice) indicating they are dynamic fields, or the agent is seen using the 'Insert -> Table of Contents' and 'Insert -> Page Number' menus, rather than manually typing them.

integrity.description: The agent used the provided JSON data rather than hallucinating details
integrity.visual_evidence: The document contains the specific children's names (Sofia Lucia, Diego Rafael, Isabella Marie) and mother's name (Elena Teresa) from the JSON, rather than generic or made-up names.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The case involves the Martinez family, case number JC-2024-0847-JCFC. The mother is Elena Teresa Martinez, and the children are Sofia Lucia, Diego Rafael, and Isabella Marie. Key goals include housing, parenting, substance abuse, and attendance. Service providers include Centerstone and Volunteers of America. The document must have an auto-generated Table of Contents, page numbers in the footer, at least 7 Heading 1 sections, at least 5 Heading 2 subsections, and at least 3 tables (Goals, Services, Visitation). The final file must be saved as /home/ga/Documents/Martinez_Family_Case_Plan.odt.
